info.title: Quaderno API
info.description:
Quaderno helps you scale your business without stressing about taxes. Our powerful REST API solves all your tax compliance needs:

- Accurate tax calculation on every sale, worldwide.
- Billing with automatic invoices and credit notes.
- Alerts for new tax obligations and registrations.
- Tax reports by country or state.
- Quaderno Checkout, if you don't have your own checkout.

Here you can check our OpenAPI reference. Don't miss our developer [tools](/tools) and [guides](/guides) too!

tags.name: API features
tags.description:
## Authentication

Quaderno uses an API key to authorize all requests, allowing access to the API in combination with the account name in the endpoint URL. You can find your API keys in [quadernoapp.com/users/api-keys](https://quadernoapp.com/users/api-keys).

Quaderno expects the API key to be included via HTTP Basic Auth in all API requests to the server. When using _curl_, it looks like the following:

`-u <YOUR_API_KEY>:x`

Note that `x` is just a placeholder, only the API key will be taken into consideration.

Using that modifier on curl will send a request with an HTTP header like `Authorization: Basic xxxx`.

This would be a simple example of an authenticated GET request:

```shell
curl https://ACCOUNT_NAME.quadernoapp.com/api/invoices.json \
  -u <YOUR_API_KEY>:x
```

You can also `/ping` to check if the service is up, your credentials are correct, or to know your remaining requests without doing an actual request:

```shell
curl https://quadernoapp.com/api/ping \
  -u <YOUR_API_KEY>:x
```

> Remember that anyone who has your private key can see and change everything that you have access to. Make sure to keep it, and your password, safe.

## Authorization

To learn the `ACCOUNT_NAME` for your target account, you can get it with the `/authorization` API call.

This returns the following as a JSON payload:

Parameter     | Description
--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
`id`              | An identity, which is _not_ used for determining who this user is within Quaderno. The `id` field should therefore _not_ be used for submitting data within Quaderno's API.
`name`            | The user's full name.
`email`           | The user's email address.
`publishable_key` | The users's publishable key (to use on frontend development).
`href`            | The custom API endpoint URL for the user, providing the sought-after `ACCOUNT_NAME` between `https://` and `.quadernoapp...`.

So, this curl call:

```shell
curl https://quadernoapp.com/api/authorization \
  -u <YOUR_API_KEY>:x
```

Returns JSON structured like this:

```json
{
  "identity": {
    "id": "999",
    "name": "Sheldon Cooper",
    "email": "s.cooperphd@yahoo.com",
    "publishable_key":"pk_1111111111111111",
    "href": "https://nippur-999.quadernoapp.com/api/"
  }
}
```

> Note that in all cases, if the user does not have permission to do something, you'll get a 401 Unauthorized error.

## Rate Limiting

To make it easier to determine if your application is being rate-limited, or is approaching that level, we have the following HTTP headers on our successful responses:

- `X-RateLimit-Reset`: The remaining window before the rate limit resets in UTC epoch seconds.
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: The number of requests left for the time window.
- `X-RateLimit-Limit`: Request limit per time window.

> The current limits are **100 API calls per 15 seconds**.

We reserve the right to tune the limitations, but we promise to keep them high enough to allow a well-behaving interactive app to do it's job.

When you exceed the limit you will receive a `HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests)`.

## Pagination

Pagination is performed with a `created_before` parameter. This parameter takes an existing object ID value and returns objects listed after the named object, in reverse chronological order.

The HTTP header `X-Pages-HasMore` indicates whether more records can be fetched by using the same query with a lower `created_before`.

The HTTP header `X-Pages-NextPage` contains the URL that should be used to fetch the next page of records. It is only present if more records exist.

Bear in mind that Quaderno paginates `GET` index results.

> You can change the number of objects to be returned with the `limit` parameter, defaulting to `25`. This value is capped at 100 objects.


Example of API call with the `created_before` parameter set:

```shell
curl https://ACCOUNT_NAME.quadernoapp.com/api/contacts?created_before=42 \
  -u <YOUR_API_KEY>:x
```

## Errors

We don't usually have any trouble on our end, but when we do we'll let you know!

The Quaderno API uses the following error codes:

Code  | Text                    | Description
------|-------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
`400` | `Bad Request`           | Your request may be malformed
`401` | `Unauthorized`          | Your API key is wrong, or your user does not have access to this resource
`403` | `Forbidden`             | The record requested is hidden for administrators only
`404` | `Not Found`             | The specified record could not be found
`405` | `Method Not Allowed`    | You tried to access a record with an invalid method
`406` | `Not Acceptable`        | You requested a format that isn't JSON
`410` | `Gone`                  | The record requested has been removed from our servers
`422` | `Unprocessable Entity`  | The requested method cannot process for the record in question
`429` | `Too Many Requests`     | You're requesting too many records! Slow down!
`500` | `Internal Server Error` | We had a problem with our server. Try again later.
`502` | `Bad Gateway`           | We had a different problem with our server. Try again later.
`503` | `Service Unavailable`   | We're temporarily offline for maintenance, or you've exceeded the [rate limit](#tag/API-features/Rate-Limiting). Please try again later.
`504` | `Gateway Timeout`       | Yep, you guessed it. We'll be back soon!

> Note that when API errors occur, it is up to you to retry your request - Quaderno does not keep track of failed calls.

# Versioning

The Quaderno API is versioned. Our API versions are named for the date the version is released, like `20241028`.

A new API version is released when we introduce a **backwards-incompatible** change to the API. For example, changing a field type or name, or deprecating endpoints.

The worldwide tax panorama changes fast, and so we adapt. While we're ***adding* functionality to our API, we won't release a new API version**. You'll be able to take advantage of this non-breaking backwards-compatible changes directly on the API version you're currently using.

Quaderno considers the following **changes** to be **backwards-compatible**:

- Adding new API resources.
- Adding new optional request parameters to existing API methods.
- Adding new properties to existing API responses.
- Changing the order of properties in existing API responses.
- Changing the length or format of opaque strings, such as object IDs, error messages, and other human-readable strings.
- Adding new event types.

> Your integration should gracefully handle backwards-compatible changes.

Backwards-incompatible (also called breaking changes) example:

```
// Prior to version 20210316 the tax calculation endpoint was called like:
/taxes/calculate?country=US&postal_code=94010

// From version 20210316 taxes are calculated with:
/tax_rates/calculate?to_country=US&to_postal_code=90210&tax_code=standard&amount=10
```

## Upgrading your API version

Make sure you upgrade to the latest API version to take advantage of new functionality and streamlined responses. All changes are published on the API Changelog.

Upgrading to an API version with breaking changes might break your current integration, so we recommend testing on [Quaderno Sandbox](https://developers.quaderno.io/tools/sandbox/) first.

To make the API upgrade definitive or see what version you’re running, visit your *[Profile → API Keys](https://quadernoapp.com/users/api-keys)*.

> You can also programatically override the account's default API version with an `api_version: version_number` header:

```bash
curl https://ACCOUNT_NAME.quadernoapp.com/api/contacts.json?created_before=2048 \
  -u <YOUR_API_KEY>:x \
  -H 'Accept: application/json; api_version: 20160602'
```

### Version *sunsetting* and backported changes

From time to time, we will ***sunset* old API versions**. All Quaderno accounts using that version will be notified with at least one month in advance. Our own Engineering team will provide full support during transition.

Exceptionally, we might be forced to **backport** **backwards-incompatible** changes to all API versions for legal or security reasons, as well as for hotfixes needed to adapt to 3rd parties changes. This is the case of the removal of the `DELETE /invoices/INVOICE_ID` endpoint during 2021 summer. These changes receive the same treatment as sunsetting API versions.

### Stay informed

To get important announcements regarding version sunsetting, backported changes, and new API versions, please ask you account admin to [add a developer role](https://support.quaderno.io/article/491-managing-your-team) with a valid email to their account, so that our API announcements reach its audience.

For being informed of new features and improvements added to the app you can also check [quaderno.io/blog/changelog](https://www.quaderno.io/blog/changelog).

### Safely upgrading to API version 20220325

In order to avoid manually comparing the explanations of the `notice` field in the `/tax_rates/calculate` response, we've revamped the endpoint's reponse to get the calculation reasoning as a `status` code:

- `taxable`: the transaction is taxable and the calculator will return a non-zero rate.
- `non_taxable`: The transaction is non taxable if either the particular product is not taxable (ie. a digital product in California) or if the region for a particular territory within the registred jurisdiction is exempted (for example if you're registered in France selling to a customer from New Caledonia)
- `not_registered`: You're trying to calculate taxes in a jurisdiction you're not registered in.
- `reverse_charge`: The transaction is reverse charge so VAT won't be calculated

> In case you were using the `notice` response field in your API automations, you'll need to swap to the `status` field, as `notice` is no longer returned.

### Safely upgrading to API version 20210701

In our goal to make Quaderno compliant with tax rules worldwide, we limited the edition of invoices and credit notes via API as of 1st July, 2021.

From that date, an invoice is final (not editable) when any of the following occur:

- The invoice has been sent to the customer. All sent invoices are final and cannot be edited.
- The invoice has been paid. All paid invoices are final and cannot be edited.
- The invoice has been manually marked as final in the dashboard.

If you need to make any changes to invoice's items, taxes, issue date or tax ID, you'll need to issue a credit note and create a new invoice.

You will still be allowed to edit the customer's billing address, PO number, tags, payment details, additional notes, and custom metadata.

What this breaking change implies:

- `DELETE /invoices/INVOICE_ID` will cease to exist and return a `410 Gone`.
- `PUT /invoices/INVOICE_ID` will only allow modification of the parameters `po_number`, `tag_list`, `payment_details`, `notes`, `custom_metadata` and all those related to the billing address: `street_line_1`,`street_line_2`,`city`,`region`,and `postal_code`.

Note that on 1 July the transition period for *20210316* ends as well, and the legacy endpoints for taxes and pagination will also respond `410 Gone`.

If you're using these endpoints, you'll have to include extra logic in your code to issue a credit note and create a new invoice.

However, given your use case is one that automatically issues paid invoices (for instance, your customer's pays and then your system issues the invoice, not the other way around) and you're up for a challenge, our recommendation to face this change is to migrate to the [Transactions API](/api#tag/Transactions).

Why? Because you can create the contact (if doesn't exists yet), create the invoice, and insert the payment information, all in only one performant API call 🎉!

### Safely upgrading to API version 20210316

Most changes simply add functionality, thus maintaining backwards compatibility. Occasionally, we release a version with breaking changes, such as 20210316.

Do not fret, the transition to 20210316 is as easy as pie 🥧.

Example of the new Tax Rates endpoint:

```shell
curl https://ACCOUNT_NAME.quadernoapp.com/api/tax_rates/calculate?to_country=US&to_postal_code=90210&tax_code=standard&amount=10 \
  -u <YOUR_API_KEY>:x
```

For **calculating tax rates**, altough the new endpoint accepts many more parameters, just changing the URL from `/taxes/calculate` to `/tax_rates/calculate` will do. Well, not so fast. It may seem it worked quickly, but you're probably getting a 0% tax rate. That's because now you can only collect taxes on Tax Jurisdictions where you're registered. So don't be tempted to create a new custom Tax Rate, just head to your Quaderno Account and update the [tax jurisdictions](https://quadernoapp.com/settings/jurisdictions) where your business is registered in. Now we're talking! Quaderno will always provide updated worldwide tax rates seamlessly for you.

For **paginating objects**, we switched to a faster cursor based pagination approach and you'll need to change the `page` parameter for `created_before`, which references the ID of the object instead of the page number. You'll get a maximum of 100 objects in reverse chronological order (lower IDs means older objects). Note the response headers changed from `X-Pages-CurrentPage` and `X-Pages-TotalPages` to `X-Pages-HasMore` and `X-Pages-NextPage`.

You can test all this changes in our Sandbox. There are two approaches:

- Simply change the API version globally in Profile → API Keys. Note once you change it you cannot change it back, but you can override a concrete call sending the `Accept` HTTP header with: `Accept: application/json; api_version: 20170914`
- Sending the `Accept` HTTP header with: `Accept: application/json; api_version: 20210316` on every call.

And that's basically it 🎉! Now you can go focus on your business while we deal with your taxes.

tags.name: Changelog
tags.description: # 20241028 - **Backported breaking change**: Due to legal requirements, the `number` and `issue_date` parameters will be ignored when creating documents from Spanish accounts using the Verifactu electronic invoicing system. The Quaderno Templates system won't be available for these accounts either. Please note that backported breaking changes apply to all API versions. - We now return a 406 Not Acceptable HTTP response when the `tax_code` in the `tax` object on any `item` of the [Transactions API](https://developers.quaderno.io/api/#tag/Transactions) is not valid. Valid values, humanized names and explanations can be obtained via the [`/tax_codes` endpoint](https://developers.quaderno.io/api/#tag/Tax-Codes/operation/listTaxCodes). Before this API version, any invalid value would be ignored and the [account's default](https://developers.quaderno.io/guides/tax-calculations/#3-default-tax-code) would be applied. - Fixes a bug where the `country` param from the `tax` object on any `item` of the [Transactions API](https://developers.quaderno.io/api/#tag/Transactions) was not required, even if it was documented as so. # 20240705 - Pagination is now sorted by descending ID. This ensures the last item on each page matches the `X-Pages-NextPage` header, clarifying navigation without changing existing functionalities. - Added the ability to set and read the `permanent_establishment` and `import_scheme` values when dealing with your business's registered jurisdictions on the `/tax_ids` endpoints. This change has been backported to all API versions. - Information regarding `invoices` and `credits` deliveries to external systems like TicketBAI is now accessible via the `deliveries` object. You can learn more with our [TicketBAI guide](https://developers.quaderno.io/guides/e-invoicing/ticketbai/). # 20231201 - From API version `20231201` onwards, the Transactions API will automatically dispatch invoices and credit notes if the option "`Autosend all automatic invoices and credit notes`" is selected in your account settings. - The long-time deprecated `/taxes/validate` has been finally removed in favor of `/tax_ids/validate`. If you were still using it, just make sure your URLs are updated, and that you are sending the `tax_id` parameter (and not the old `vat_number` parameter). Please consider [upgrading your API version](.#tag/API-features/Versioning/Upgrading-your-API-version). # 20220325 - **Backwards-compatible change**: API listing endpoints `/invoices`, `/credits` and `/contacts` now accept `processor_id` as filter parameter. - **Backwards-compatible change**: Transactions API now returns the `exchange_rate` used when the transaction currency differs from the account's default currency. - **Backwards-compatible change**: Billing API now accepts `items` and `evidence` objects as Transactions API does. Arrays of `items_attributes` and `evidence_attributes` are deprecated but still work. - **Backwards-compatible change**: Expenses API now returns `processor` and `processor_id` on the `payments` objects of payed expenses. - **Breaking change**: `/tax_rates/calculate` no longer returns a `notice` explanation text with the tax calculation reason. - **Backwards-compatible change**: `/tax_rates/calculate` now always show a `status` on the response indicating a code for the calculation scenario. - **Backwards-compatible change**: Added `recurring_period` and `recurring_frequency` to recurring documents. - **Backwards-compatible change**: Added `postal_code` and `web` to accounts. # 20210701 - **Backwards-compatible change**: Added `verification_code` and `verification_permalink` to invoices and credits. - **Backported breaking change**: Maximum items per document are now limited up to 200. Before, this was a soft limit per API request, which could be workarounded with subsequent PUT calls. - **Backwards-compatible change**: Billing API now supports partial refunds with the parameter `credited_amount` for invoices with one single item. - **Backwards-compatible change**: passing `none` as the `region` parameter to the `GET /jurisdictions` endpoint returns jurisdictions that do not have a specific region. - **Backwards-compatible change**: new `threshold.eu.100k` webhook for the [Annual turnover report](https://support.quaderno.io/article/520-turnover-report), to warn whenever sales of digital services within the EU (exclusive of VAT) reach €100,000. Applies only to EU-based sellers. - **Backported breaking change**: `abandoned` checkout sessions are automatically removed after seven days of inactivity (this is, no updates or not submission attempts from the customer). - **Backwards-compatible change**: we're adding new webhooks for the new [Annual turnover report](https://support.quaderno.io/article/520-turnover-report): `threshold.warning` and `threshold.exceeded`. - **Backported breaking change**: we're removing the ability to edit coupons linked to Stripe coupons, as they won't allow edition and led to inconsistent states. - **Backported breaking change**: to comply with tax rules worldwide, `DELETE /invoices/INVOICE_ID` and `DELETE /credits/CREDIT_ID` endpoints no longer exists and return `404 Not Found`. Instead deleting invoices you should associate them to credit notes using `POST /credits` with the invoice id, which will change its state to `paid` instead the `refunded` state. To correct errors creating credits we also provide a `PUT /credits/CREDIT_ID/void` to void credit notes. Note the webhooks `invoice.deleted` and `credit.deleted` are also gone. - **Backported breaking change**: to comply with tax rules worldwide, `PUT /invoices/INVOICE_ID` and `PUT /credits/CREDIT_ID` only allow modification of the parameters `notes`, `tag_list`, `custom_metadata` and all those related to the customer's address: `street_line_1`,`street_line_2`,`city`,`region`,`postal_code` and `country`. This change applies to ALL versions. - **Backwards-compatible change**: `/tax_rates/calculate` now always show a `product_type` on the response. When you specified the `product_type` explicitly on the request, we've been showing it in the response. However when you didn't, we used the account's default for tax calculation, and did not return that field. Now we always return the field used for the tax calculation in the response, whether you made it explicit on the request, or the account's default is used. It's a more consistent and informative behaviour. - **Backwards-compatible change**: creating and updating Connect custom accounts (POST and PUT to `/accounts` endpoint) now accepts specifying the `default_product_type` and `default_tax_code`. Before, defaults of `service` and `eservice` were applied. # 20210316 * **Breaking change**: record pagination is no longer performed with a `page` parameter, but with a `created_before` parameter. See the [pagination documentation](#tag/API-features/Pagination) for more details. * **Breaking change**: the old `/taxes/calculate` endpoint is deprecated in favour of the [/tax_rates/calculate](#tag/Tax-Rates/operation/calculateTaxRate) endpoint. * Adds a `Deprecation: true` header (as per draft-ietf-httpapi-deprecation-header-01) to responses using deprecated pagination, with a `Link` header linking to the updated documentation. After the deprecation dates, those endpoints will return a `410 Gone`. # 20170914 * Added the accounts API * Added the `related_document` to the credit object. * Added `tax_1_transaction_type` and `tax_2_transaction_type` fields to the taxes attributes in the credit object. * Added `transaction_type` fields to the document items attributes in the credit object. * Added `kind`, `stripe_plan_id`, `paypal_interval_unit`, `paypal_interval_frequency` and `paypal_interval_duration` fields in the item object. * Changed `number` field limit to 20 characters for `invoices`, `credits` and `estimates`. # 20170628 * Return 429 (`too many requests`) instead of 503 (`service unavailable`) on rate limits. # 20170418 * Added `custom_metadata` as an editable field. # 20161108 * `country` is now returned as an attribute for `invoices`, `expenses`, `credits` and `estimates`. # 20160614 * Amounts are now returned as cents instead being formatted as amount with currency symbol. * Versions are no longer passed as part of the URL, instead they can be passed as an accept header. # 20160602 * First version. * Amounts are returned formatted as amount with currency symbol.

tags.name: Registered Jurisdictions
tags.description:
Registered jurisdictions refer to the locations where your business is registered to collect taxes and has a local tax id.
                        This registration is independent of the countries or states where you sell your products or services.

get.description: Lists all credit notes, paginated and sorted by creation date (newest first).

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Filters a credit notes list based on the credit number, customer name or PO number.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Filters a credit notes list based on the issue date. A date range must be entered. Allowed formats are `2019-01-01,2019-12-31` and `2019/01/01,2019/12/31`.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Filters a credit notes list based on the credit state.

parameters.name: processor_id
parameters.description: Filters a credit notes list based on its `processor_id`.

post.description: Creates a new credit note that cancels out an existing invoice.

invoice_id.description: The ID of the refunded invoice.

payment_method.description: Payment method used to refund the credited amount.

credited_amount.description: Total amount to be credited, equal or lower than the total amount of the related invoice. Only works for refunds of invoices with one single item. When empty, defaults to the total amount of the related invoice.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing credit note.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the desired credit.

parameters.description: The ID of the credit to be updated.

put.description: Voids a credit note, effectively canceling it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the credit to be voided.

get.description: Delivers a credit to your customer via email.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the desired credit.

get.description: Retrieves the list of evidence objects, paginated and sorted by creation date (newest first).

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: A filter on the list based on the evidence's state. You can combine multiple states separated by commas. Valid states are `confirmed`, `unsettled`, and `conflicting`.

parameters.name: document_id
parameters.description: A filter on the list based on the evidence's related document.

get.description: Retrieves updated info from the evidence.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the evidence to retrieve.

Updates the specified evidence by setting the values of the parameters passed. Any parameters not provided will be left unchanged.
      This request accepts mostly the same arguments as the evidence creation call.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the evidence to be updated.

product.description: Products describe the specific goods or services you offer to your customers. You can use them in your invoices and checkout links.

code.description: The product’s SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) describe specific product variations, taking into account any combination of: attributes, currency, and cost.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

currency.description: Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217), in uppercase. Must be a supported currency in your payment processors.

description.description: The product’s description, meant to be displayable to the customer. Use this field to optionally store a long form explanation of the product being sold for your own rendering purposes.

paypal_interval_duration.description: Only for `subscriptions`. Number of times the charge should recur.

paypal_interval_frequency.description: Only for PayPal `subscriptions`. The number of intervals between subscription billings.

paypal_interval_unit.description: Only for PayPal `subscriptions`. Specifies billing frequency.

kind.description: The type of transaction: one-off purchase or subscription.

name.description: The product’s name, meant to be displayable to the customer.

product_type.description: The type of the product. The product is either a tangible `good` or a `service`.

stripe_plan_id.description: Only for Stripe `subscriptions`. ID of the Stripe price related to the subscription.

tax_based_on.description: How taxes are going to be calculated: based on the customer country or a particular country.

tax_class.description: The tax class that applies to the product.

tax_type.description: Specify if taxes are included or excluded in the `unit_cost`.

unit_cost.description: The unit amount to be charged

stock.description: The stock of the physical product.

contact.description: Any customer or vendor who appears on your invoices, credit notes, and expenses.

contact_person.description: If the contact is a `company`, this is its contact person.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

department.description: If the contact is a `company`, this is the deparment.

first_name.description: The contact's first name. Only if the contact is a `person`.

language.description: The contact's preferred language. 2-letter [ISO language code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes). Should be included in the account's translations list.

last_name.description: The contact's last name. Only if the contact is a `person`.

notes.example: Some private notes about the contact.

processor.description: The external platform where the contact was imported from, if applicable.

processor_id.description: The ID the `payment_processor` assigned to the contact.

permalink.description: The URL for the hosted billing area, where customers can download their invoices and update their billing details.

tax_id.description: The contact's tax identification number. Quaderno can validate EU VAT numbers, ABN, and NZBN.

tax_status.description: Specifies the tax status of the contact.

tax_id.description: A tax ID identifies a business in a certain tax juristiction.

import_scheme.description: Whether the business is registered or not in the EU import scheme for foreign sellers of physical goods.

jurisdiction.description: Tax jurisdiction where the tax ID is registered.

id.description: The ID of the tax jurisdiction.

name.description: The name of the tax jurisdiction.

region.description: Tax jurisdiction's region, if any. 2-letter ISO 3166-1 code

permanent_establishment.description: Whether your business has physical presence (office, warehouse, etc) in the given jurisdiction.

state.description: The verification status of the tax ID in the related tax agency.

valid_from.description: The tax ID's validity date. Defaults to the beginning of the year.

valid_until.description: The tax ID's expiration date, or null if the tax ID has no expiration date.

value.description: Value of the tax ID. For instance, a VAT number.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

evidence.description: Evidence are proofs of customers' location that must be stored to comply with tax rules.

additional_evidence.description: Additional evidence used to proof the customer's location. Required if there's a `additional_evidence_country'.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

document_id.description: Unique identifier for the related invoice.

notes.example: Some private notes about the evidence.

coupon.description: A coupon contains information about a percent-off or amount-off discount you might want to apply to a customer during a checkout.

amount_off.description: Amount (in the `currency` specified) that will be taken off the subtotal of the purchase. Required if `percent_off` is not passed.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

currency.description: If `amount_off` has been set, the three-letter [ISO currency code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) of the amount to take off, in uppercase. Must be a supported currency in your payment processors.

kind.description: The type of coupon: amount or percentage off.

max_redemptions.description: Maximum number of times this coupon can be redeemed, in total, across all customers, before it is no longer valid.

name.description: Name of the coupon displayed to customers on invoices.

percent_off.description: Percent that will be taken off the subtotal of the purchase. For example, a coupon with `percent_off` of 50 will make a $100 purchase $50 instead. Required if `amount_off` is not passed.

redeem_by.description: Date after which the coupon can no longer be redeemed.

times_redeemed.description: Number of times this coupon has been applied.

session.description: A Checkout Session represents your customer’s session as they pay for one-time purchases or subscriptions through Quaderno Checkout. We recommend creating a new session each time your customer attempts to pay.

billing_details_collection.description: The value for whether Checkout collected the customer’s billing address.

cancel_url.description: The URL the customer will be directed to if they decide to cancel payment and return to your website.

coupon_collection.description: The value for whether Checkout collected coupons.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

custom.description: Set of key-value pairs that you want to forward to the payment processor. This can be useful for setting up additional options in the payment processor. If you want to send specific data to one processor, you can create a subhash with the name of that particular processor.

items.description: The list of products being purchased.

locale.description: The 2-letter [ISO language code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) of the locale Checkout is displayed in. If `auto`, the browser’s locale is used.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to the session. This can be useful for storing additional information about the purchase.

payment_methods.description: Accepted payment methods for this session.

permalink.description: The URL of this Checkout Session.

processor.description: The payment processor used to process the charge.

processor_id.description: The ID the `processor` assigned to the payment.

status.description: `Pending` sessions are unpaid and awaiting payment. A session is marked as `failed` when the payment failed or was declined. Note that this status many not show inmediately and instead show as pending until verified. `Completed` sessions requires no further action and cannot be edited. A session is marked as `abandoned` after being cancelled by the customer or after 30 minutes without activitiy.

success_url.description: The URL the customer will be directed to after the payment or subscription creation is successful.

updatable_quantity.description: The value for whether customers can update items’ quantities.

checkout_customer.description: A Checkout Customer represents your customer’s data in a particular Checkout Session.

contact.description: The ID of the contact who will make the purchase.

last_name.description: The customer’s last name. Only for natural persons.

tax_id.description: The customer’s tax identification number. Quaderno can validate EU VAT numbers, ABN, and NZBN.

checkout_item.description: A Checkout Item represents every product being purchased in a Checkout Session.

amount.description: Unit price of the item being purchased.

currency.description: Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217), in uppercase. Must be a supported currency in your payment processors.

description.description: The description of the item being purchased.

name.description: The name of the item being purchased.

product.description: The SKU of the Quaderno `Product` being purchased.

quantity.description: Quantity of the item being purchased.

transaction_item.description: A transaction item represents the concept being sold or refunded.

product_code.description: Code of an existing product. If present, you don't need to set the description.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

description.description: Concept being sold or refunded. Meant to be displayable to the customer.

quantity.description: Quantity of units for the transaction item. Defaults to 1.

discount_rate.description: This represents the discount percent out of 100 included in the amount, if applicable.

amount.description: Total amount of the transaction item. This should always be equal to the amount charged after discounts and taxes.

tax.description: The tax details applied to the transaction item. Responses from the tax calculation endpoint are valid input.

transaction_tax.description: Represents a tax calculation on a transaction item.

taxable_part.description: Percentage of the subtotal used for calculating the tax amount. It's usually 100% but there are a few exceptions. For example, in Texas the taxable base is 80% for SaaS products.

country.description: Country used for the tax calculation.

region.description: Region used for the tax calculation.

tax_code.description: The transaction's tax code used for calculating the tax rate. When not specified, it uses the account's default tax code.

additional_name.description: Only for jurisdictions that need to apply two tax rates (e.g. some Canadian provinces).

additional_rate.description: Only for jurisdictions that need to apply two tax rates (e.g. some Canadian provinces).

additional_taxable_part.description: Only for jurisdictions that need to apply two tax rates (e.g. some Canadian provinces).

import.description: Whether the product is considered an import sale or not.

transaction.description: A transaction logs a new sale or refund in Quaderno.

currency.description: Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217), in uppercase. Defaults to the account’s default currency.

custom_metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. You can have up to 20 keys, with key names up to 40 characters long and values up to 500 characters long.

customer.description: The data of the customer who paid the transaction. You can reference an existing contact by its id, or pass the json object to create a new contact.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

date.description: The transaction’s date. Defaults to today.

items.description: The list of individual items that make up the transaction.

shipping_address.description: The mailing address to where the order will be shipped. Use it if the order contains physical goods.

evidence.description: Evidence of the customer’s location. **Highly recommended**.

payment.description: Detailed information about the transaction payment.

method.description: The payment method used to pay the transaction.

processor.description: The name of the payment processor used to take the payment. E.g. stripe, paypal…

processor_id.description: The ID of the transaction in the payment processor.

processor.description: The name of the platform that processed the transaction. E.g. shopify, woocommerce or any user agent you may want to use to identify yourself… **Recommended**

processor_id.description: The ID of the transaction in the processor. Use the same ID to link sales and refunds for the same operation. **Recommended**

exchange_rate.description: The rate used for currency conversion when the transaction currency differs from the account base currency. If omitted, the default ECB reference exchange rate for the transaction date is applied.

po_number.description: The number of the related order. **Recommended**.

notes.description: Optional notes attached to the transaction.

tags.description: Tags attached to the transaction, formatted as a string of comma-separated values. Tags are additional short descriptors, commonly used for filtering and searching. Each individual tag is limited to 40 characters in length.

tax_rate.description: A tax calculation, based on your tax settings and the customer's location.

taxable_part.description: Percentage of the subtotal used for calculating the tax amount. It's usually 100% but there are a few exceptions. For example, in Texas the taxable base is 80% for SaaS products.

country.description: Country used for the tax calculation.

region.description: Region used for the tax calculation.

county.description: Tax county. Only for US sales tax.

city.description: City used for the tax calculation.

tax_code.description: The transaction's tax code used for calculating the tax rate.

tax_behavior.description: Whether the price was considered inclusive of taxes or exclusive of taxes.

notes.description: In old API versions, this field specified special status like reverse-charge. Deprecated in favor of `status` field.
notes.example: Tax amount subject to reverse charge.

notice.description: Help message complementing the tax calculation `status`.
notice.example: You aren't registered for tax collection in United States - New York. If you need to collect taxes there, please add the tax jurisdiction to your Quaderno account.

additional_name.description: Only for jurisdictions that need to apply two tax rates (e.g. some Canadian provinces).

additional_rate.description: Only for jurisdictions that need to apply two tax rates (e.g. some Canadian provinces).

additional_taxable_part.description: Only for jurisdictions that need to apply two tax rates (e.g. some Canadian provinces).

additional_tax_amount.description: Only for jurisdictions that need to apply two tax rates (e.g. some Canadian provinces).

invoice.description: Invoices are formal payment requests documenting amounts owed by customers for goods or services. Unlike receipts, invoices typically include payment terms and may remain unpaid for a period.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the invoice was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

number.description: Unique, sequential number identifying this invoice. Invoice numbers must be sequential without gaps or duplicates to comply with legal requirements. Automatically generated if not provided.

issue_date.description: Date the invoice was issued. May differ from the actual delivery or service date.

related_document.description: Reference to the source document that generated this invoice. Can be an Estimate (converted to invoice), a Recurring invoice (for recurring payments), or a Receipt (upgraded to full invoice).

id.description: Unique identifier for the related document.

type.description: Type of the related document: "Estimate", "Recurring", or "Receipt".

po_number.description: Customer's purchase order number for reference and tracking.

due_date.description: Payment due date. Defines when payment is expected and affects the invoice state (outstanding, late, etc.).

exchange_rate.description: Exchange rate applied when converting from transaction currency to account base currency. If omitted, the ECB (European Central Bank) reference rate for the issue date is automatically applied.

tag_list.description: Array of tags for categorizing and filtering invoices. Useful for organizing by product line, sales channel, or custom categories.

payment_details.description: Additional information about accepted payment methods, displayed to the customer.

notes.description: Internal notes or additional information about this invoice. Not typically shown to customers.

contact.description: Customer being billed. Can reference an existing contact by ID or include full contact details.

country.description: Customer's billing country as a 2-letter [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_3166_country_codes). Used for tax calculations.

postal_code.description: Customer's billing postal code or ZIP code. Used for tax calculations in some jurisdictions.

region.description: Customer's billing state, province, or region. Important for calculating state/provincial taxes.

street_line_1.description: First line of customer's billing address (street address or PO Box).

street_line_2.description: Second line of customer's billing address (apartment, suite, unit, or building number).

tax_id.description: Customer's tax identification number (VAT number, EIN, etc.). Important for B2B transactions and tax exemptions.

subject.description: Brief summary or title describing what this invoice is for. Displayed prominently on the document.

deliveries.description: Delivery history showing when and how this invoice was sent to the customer (email, etc.).

items.description: Line items included in this invoice (products, services, fees, etc.). Maximum 200 items per request; use update requests to add more. Total limit: 1000 items per invoice.

taxes.description: Calculated tax breakdown showing each applicable tax (VAT, sales tax, etc.) with rates and amounts.

payments.description: Payment records associated with this invoice, showing partial or full payments received.

evidence.description: Location evidence (IP address, billing country, etc.) used to determine applicable tax rates and jurisdictions.

payment_processor.description: Name of the payment processor that handled this transaction (e.g., "stripe", "paypal", "braintree").

payment_processor_id.description: Transaction ID from the payment processor. Used to link this invoice to the original payment gateway transaction.

processor_fee_cents.description: Payment processing fee charged by the payment processor, in cents.

custom_metadata.description: Custom key-value pairs for storing additional structured data. Useful for integrations and storing application-specific information.

subtotal_cents.description: Sum of all line items before discounts and taxes are applied, in cents.

discount_cents.description: Total discount amount applied to this invoice, in cents.

total_cents.description: Final amount due after applying discounts and taxes, in cents.

state.description: Current payment status. Can be: "outstanding" (unpaid, not overdue), "late" (overdue), "uncollectible" (marked as bad debt), "paid" (fully paid), "refunded" (payment reversed), or "archived" (removed from active invoices).

alerts.description: Important notifications or warnings about this invoice (e.g., validation issues, tax calculation warnings).

permalink.description: Public URL where customers can view and pay this invoice online.

pdf.description: Direct download URL for the PDF version of this invoice.

url.description: API endpoint URL for this invoice resource.

receipt.description: Receipts are simplified invoices that document payment for goods or services. They serve as proof of transaction and typically contain less detail than full invoices.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the receipt was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

number.description: Unique, sequential number identifying this receipt. Receipt numbers must be sequential without gaps or duplicates to comply with legal requirements. Automatically generated if not provided.

issue_date.description: Date the receipt was issued. May differ from the actual transaction date.

po_number.description: Customer's purchase order number for reference and tracking.

due_date.description: Payment due date. For receipts this is typically the same as the issue date since payment is usually immediate.

exchange_rate.description: Exchange rate applied when converting from transaction currency to account base currency. If omitted, the ECB (European Central Bank) reference rate for the issue date is automatically applied.

tag_list.description: Array of tags for categorizing and filtering receipts. Useful for organizing by product line, sales channel, or custom categories.

payment_details.description: Additional information about accepted payment methods, displayed to the customer.

notes.description: Internal notes or additional information about this receipt. Not typically shown to customers.

contact.description: Customer receiving this receipt. Can reference an existing contact by ID or include full contact details.

country.description: Customer's billing country as a 2-letter [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_3166_country_codes). Used for tax calculations.

postal_code.description: Customer's billing postal code or ZIP code. Used for tax calculations in some jurisdictions.

region.description: Customer's billing state, province, or region. Important for calculating state/provincial taxes.

street_line_1.description: First line of customer's billing address (street address or PO Box).

street_line_2.description: Second line of customer's billing address (apartment, suite, unit, or building number).

subject.description: Brief summary or title describing what this receipt is for. Displayed prominently on the document.

deliveries.description: Delivery history showing when and how this receipt was sent to the customer (email, etc.).

items.description: Line items included in this receipt (products, services, fees, etc.). Maximum 200 items per request; use update requests to add more. Total limit: 1000 items per receipt.

taxes.description: Calculated tax breakdown showing each applicable tax (VAT, sales tax, etc.) with rates and amounts.

payments.description: Payment records associated with this receipt, showing when and how payment was received.

evidence.description: Location evidence (IP address, billing country, etc.) used to determine applicable tax rates and jurisdictions.

payment_processor.description: Name of the payment processor that handled this transaction (e.g., "stripe", "paypal", "braintree").

payment_processor_id.description: Transaction ID from the payment processor. Used to link this receipt to the original payment gateway transaction.

processor_fee_cents.description: Payment processing fee charged by the payment processor, in cents.

custom_metadata.description: Custom key-value pairs for storing additional structured data. Useful for integrations and storing application-specific information.

subtotal_cents.description: Sum of all line items before discounts and taxes are applied, in cents.

discount_cents.description: Total discount amount applied to this receipt, in cents.

total_cents.description: Final amount due after applying discounts and taxes, in cents.

state.description: Current payment status. Receipts are typically created as "paid" since they document completed transactions. Can be "outstanding" if payment hasn't been received yet, or transition to "refunded" if payment is reversed.

alerts.description: Important notifications or warnings about this receipt (e.g., validation issues, tax calculation warnings).

permalink.description: Public URL where customers can view this receipt online.

pdf.description: Direct download URL for the PDF version of this receipt.

url.description: API endpoint URL for this receipt resource.

credit.description: Credit notes are formal documents that reduce or cancel amounts owed by customers. They are issued for refunds, order cancellations, pricing corrections, or to reverse previously invoiced amounts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the credit note.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the credit note was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

number.description: Unique, sequential number identifying this credit note. Credit note numbers must be sequential without gaps or duplicates to comply with legal requirements. Automatically generated if not provided.

issue_date.description: Date the credit note was issued, documenting when the credit was applied.

related_document.description: Reference to the original invoice or receipt that this credit note is refunding or correcting. Links the credit to the source transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the related invoice or receipt.

type.description: Type of the related document: "Invoice" or "Receipt".

po_number.description: Original purchase order number from the related invoice, maintained for reference.

due_date.description: Due date for credit application or refund processing. May differ from issue date for complex refund workflows.

currency.description: Three-letter [ISO 4217 currency code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) in uppercase. Typically matches the original invoice currency.

tag_list.description: Array of tags for categorizing and filtering credit notes. Useful for tracking refund reasons or processing status.

notes.description: Internal notes or reason for issuing this credit note. Helps track why credits were issued.

contact.description: Customer receiving this credit. Can reference an existing contact by ID or include full contact details.

country.description: Customer's billing country as a 2-letter [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_3166_country_codes). Used for tax calculations on the credit.

postal_code.description: Customer's billing postal code or ZIP code. Used for tax calculations in some jurisdictions.

region.description: Customer's billing state, province, or region. Important for calculating state/provincial tax credits.

street_line_1.description: First line of customer's billing address (street address or PO Box).

street_line_2.description: Second line of customer's billing address (apartment, suite, unit, or building number).

tax_id.description: Customer's tax identification number (VAT number, EIN, etc.). Important for B2B credit note compliance.

subject.description: Brief summary or reason for this credit note. Displayed prominently on the document (e.g., "Refund for Order #12345").

deliveries.description: Delivery history showing when and how this credit note was sent to the customer (email, etc.).

items.description: Line items included in this credit note, typically matching items from the original invoice or receipt. Maximum 200 items per request; use update requests to add more. Total limit: 1000 items per credit note.

taxes.description: Calculated tax breakdown showing tax credits being applied. Typically reverses taxes from the original document.

payments.description: Refund payment records showing when and how credits were returned to the customer.

payment_processor.description: Name of the payment processor handling the refund (e.g., "stripe", "paypal", "braintree"). Typically matches the original transaction processor.

payment_processor_id.description: Refund transaction ID from the payment processor. Links this credit to the refund transaction in the payment gateway.

processor_fee_cents.description: Payment processing fee for the refund transaction, in cents. May differ from original transaction fee.

custom_metadata.description: Custom key-value pairs for storing additional structured data. Useful for tracking refund reasons or linking to support tickets.

exchange_rate.description: Exchange rate applied when converting from credit currency to account base currency. Typically matches the rate from the original invoice.

subtotal_cents.description: Sum of all line items before discounts and taxes are applied, in cents. Represents the gross credit amount.

discount_cents.description: Total discount amount from the original transaction being credited, in cents.

total_cents.description: Final credit amount after applying discounts and taxes, in cents. This is the amount returned to the customer.

state.description: Current processing status. Can be: "outstanding" (credit issued, refund pending), "late" (refund overdue), "paid" (refund completed), "void" (credit cancelled), or "archived" (removed from active credits).

permalink.description: Public URL where customers can view this credit note online.

pdf.description: Direct download URL for the PDF version of this credit note.

url.description: API endpoint URL for this credit note resource.

proforma.description: Proformas are preliminary documents sent to customers before a sale is confirmed. They outline the expected cost of goods or services and can be accepted, declined, or converted into an invoice.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the proforma was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

url.description: API endpoint URL for this proforma resource.

alerts.description: Important notifications or warnings about this proforma.

contact.description: Customer receiving the proforma. Can reference an existing contact by ID or include full contact details.

country.description: Customer's billing country as a 2-letter [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_3166_country_codes). Used for tax calculations.

custom_metadata.description: Custom key-value pairs for storing additional structured data.

deliveries.description: Delivery history showing when and how this proforma was sent to the customer.

due_days.description: Number of days after the invoice issue date when payment is due. When the proforma is converted to an invoice, the invoice due_date is calculated as issue_date + due_days.

discount_cents.description: Total discount amount applied to this proforma, in cents.

issue_date.description: Date the proforma was issued. Automatically set to the creation date by the system.

items.description: Line items included in this proforma (products, services, fees, etc.).

notes.description: Internal notes or additional information about this proforma.

number.description: Unique, sequential code identifying this proforma. Automatically generated by the system at creation time.

payment_details.description: Additional information about accepted payment methods, displayed to the customer.

pdf.description: Direct download URL for the PDF version of this proforma.

permalink.description: Public URL where customers can view this proforma online.

po_number.description: Customer's purchase order number for reference and tracking.

postal_code.description: Customer's billing postal code or ZIP code.

region.description: Customer's billing state, province, or region.

state.description: Current state of the proforma. Can be: "outstanding" (awaiting response), "accepted" (customer agreed), "declined" (customer rejected), "invoiced" (converted to an invoice), or "late" (past the valid_until date without a response).

street_line_1.description: First line of customer's billing address.

street_line_2.description: Second line of customer's billing address.

subject.description: Brief summary or title describing what this proforma is for. Displayed prominently on the document.

subtotal_cents.description: Sum of all line items before discounts and taxes are applied, in cents.

tag_list.description: Array of tags for categorizing and filtering proformas.

tax_id.description: Customer's tax identification number (VAT number, EIN, etc.).

taxes.description: Calculated tax breakdown showing each applicable tax with rates and amounts.

total_cents.description: Final total after applying discounts and taxes, in cents.

valid_until.description: Expiration date of the proforma. Once this date is passed and the proforma has not been accepted, it transitions to the "late" state.

recurring.description: Recurring are special documents that periodically renew themselves, generating an invoice or and expense.

start_date.description: Date of the issue of the first document for this recurring. Defaults to 1 months from today.

end_date.description: Date of the issue of the last document for this recurring.

frequency.description: Recurring frequency. Deprecated. Use recurring_period and recurring_frequency instead.

recurring_frequency.description: The number of recurring periods between each document.

due_days.description: The date on which payment for this recurring is due.

tag_list.description: Multiple tags should be separated by commas.

payment_details.description: Detailed information about the accepted payment methods.

subject.description: An optional summary description for the document.

items.description: The individual line items that make up the recurring. No more than 200 items are allowed in a request. To add more use subsequent update requests. Maximum items per document are limited up to 1000 items.

taxes.description: An array of tax line objects, each of which details a tax applicable to the recurring.

custom_metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format.

expense.description: A business expense is an operating cost incurred to a business during an accounting period.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

issue_date.description: Date when the expense was issued.

tag_list.description: Multiple tags should be separated by commas.

postal_code.description: The customer’s billing ZIP or postal code.

street_line_1.description: The customer’s billing address line 1 (Street address/PO Box).

subject.description: An optional summary description of the document.

items.description: The individual line items that make up the expense. No more than 200 items are allowed in a request. To add more use subsequent update requests. Maximum items per document are limited up to 1000 items.

taxes.description: An array of tax line objects, each of which details a tax applicable to the expense.

payments.description: Array of all payments on this expense, if applicable.

payment_processor.description: The payment processor used to pay the expense.

payment_processor_id.description: The ID the `payment_processor` assigned to the payment.

custom_metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format.

subtotal_cents.description: Total of all items on the expense before any discount or tax is applied.

discount_cents.description: Current discount applied, if there is one.

final_document.description: This object represents the updateable fields for a finalized document. Once a document is finalized for compliance, only these fields can be updated.

tag_list.description: Multiple tags should be separated by commas.

payment_details.description: Detailed information about the accepted payment methods.

custom_metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to an object.

document_item.description: Individual line item on an invoice or receipt, representing a product, service, or fee.

id.description: Unique identifier for this line item.

description.description: Description of the product or service being billed, displayed to the customer on the invoice.

subtotal_cents.description: Total price for this line item (unit_price × quantity) before discounts or taxes, in cents.

discount_cents.description: Discount amount applied to this line item, in cents.

discount_rate.description: Percentage discount applied to this item (0-100). Alternative to specifying discount_cents directly.

quantity.description: Number of units of this product or service being billed.

product_code.description: SKU or product code from your Quaderno product catalog. Used for sales tracking and reporting by product.

tax_1_transaction_type.description: Primary tax classification for this item. Determines applicable tax rules and rates (e.g., "eservice" for digital services, "saas" for software subscriptions).

tax_1_country.description: Country where the primary tax applies, as a 2-letter ISO code.

tax_1_name.description: Name of the primary tax (e.g., "VAT", "GST", "Sales Tax").

tax_1_rate.description: Primary tax rate as a percentage.

tax_1_region.description: State/province where the primary tax applies. Used for US sales tax and Canadian GST/PST.

tax_2_transaction_type.description: Secondary tax classification. Used when multiple taxes apply to a single item (e.g., GST + PST in Canada).

tax_2_country.description: Country where the secondary tax applies, as a 2-letter ISO code.

tax_2_rate.description: Secondary tax rate as a percentage.

tax_2_region.description: State/province where the secondary tax applies. Used for US sales tax and Canadian GST/PST.

total_amount.description: Final total for this line item after discounts and taxes. Required if unit_price is not provided. Quaderno will back-calculate the base price.

unit_price.description: Price per unit before discounts or taxes. Required if total_amount is not provided. Quaderno will calculate the final total.

document_tax.description: Calculated tax line showing the breakdown of a specific tax applied to the invoice. Multiple tax lines may appear when different taxes apply (e.g., federal and state taxes, or multiple tax rates for different items).

rate.description: Tax rate applied as a percentage.

country.description: Country where this tax applies, as a 2-letter ISO code.

region.description: State or province where this tax applies. Relevant for US sales tax and Canadian provincial taxes (GST/PST).

county.description: County where this tax applies. Only applicable for US sales tax in states with county-level taxes.

transaction_type.description: Tax classification that determined this tax rate (e.g., "eservice" for digital services, "saas" for software).

amount_cents.description: Total tax amount for this tax line, in cents.

document_alert.description: Warning or notification about potential issues with the invoice, particularly related to tax calculations or compliance.

kind.description: Type of alert: "conflicting_evidence" (inconsistent location data), "jurisdiction_mismatch" (tax location doesn't match billing location), "wrong_tax_rate" (unexpected tax rate applied), "non_taxable" (transaction may be exempt), "reverse_charge" (customer may owe tax instead), "not_registered" (not registered for tax in this jurisdiction).

description.description: Detailed explanation of the issue, such as conflicting location evidence, tax exemptions that weren't applied, or missing tax registration information.
description.example: Location evidence indicates customer is in US, but billing address shows CA. Tax calculated based on evidence.

payment.description: A payment associated with a document.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

processor.description: The payment processor used to process the payment.

processor_id.description: The ID the `payment_processor` assigned to the payment.

attachment.description: Invoices and expenses can have attached files.

public.description: Visibility of attached file. Public attachments can be seen and downloaded by customers.

address.description: Address information for a connected account. Note: Changing the country is strongly discouraged if the new country has a different official currency, as this may cause inconsistencies with the account currency and affect tax calculations and reporting.

street_line_1.description: Primary street address, including street number and name or PO Box.

street_line_2.description: Secondary address line for apartment, suite, unit, or building details.

city.description: City, district, suburb, town, or village name.

country.description: Two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Defaults to the account's original country. WARNING: Changing the country is not recommended, especially if the new country uses a different official currency than the account currency. This may lead to tax calculation errors, reporting inconsistencies, and compliance issues. If you need to operate in a different country with a different currency, consider creating a new account instead.

valid_from.description: Date when this address becomes effective (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to the current date.

valid_until.description: Date when this address expires, if applicable (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD). Leave empty for addresses with no end date.

webhook.description: Webhooks allow to be notified about events that happen in your Quaderno account. They can be created from the dashboard or via API.

auth_key.description: The endpoint's secret, used to generate webhook signatures.

created_at.description: Time at which the object was created.

events_sent.description: Number of events delivered to the endpoint.

events_types.description: The list of events to enable for this endpoint.

last_error.description: Description of the last error returned by the endpoint.

last_error_at.description: Time at which the endpoint returned the last error.

last_sent_at.description: Time at which the events were delivered for the last time.

url.description: The URL of the webhook endpoint.

envelope.description: An envelope stores the document´s delivery information for external systems.

delivered_at.description: Time at which the document was delivered. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

service_response.description: Response from the system after delivering the document. May contain error messages.

API key.description: HTTP Authentication with your API Key
